entry.title: Attention Suppliers: Update Your SAP Integration Suite, managed Gateway URLs
entry.content:
Attention SAP Business Network Suppliers!

SAP has extended the domain decommission date for SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management, and SAP Business Network. Here’s what you need to know and do before the decommission date of January 31st, 2026.

What's Happening?

The URL address you’re currently using to send transactions to SAP Business Network is changing for both Test and Production environments. After the decommission date, transactions sent via the old domains will fail.

Who's Impacted?

If you’re using SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management, or SAP Business Network for transaction exchanges, this change affects you.

What You Need to Do:

  1. Notify your IT department about these changes.
  2. VAN Providers should be informed by their customers. 
  3. Replace the current URL with the new one in your internal tool/application or via your service provider.
  4. Validate the change and regression test to ensure smooth business continuity.

Technical Domain Details:

  • The new domain names are already live, you can update them asap.
  • The current domains will be decommissioned on January 31, 2026.
  • For more specific data center details, check the full notification.

For further information, please refer to the FAQ for Suppliers.

Please ensure you implement these changes before the decommission date to avoid any disruption in services.

Thank you, SAP Team

entry.title: A journey from Netweaver 7.50 to S/4HANA - Part 3: Hitting some snags
entry.content:
Find part 1 here and part 2 here to learn more about our project in general and "what happened earlier".

Part 3 of my ongoing blog series will focus on how we planned to carry out the manual custom code conversion and how some snags derailed parts of these plans and required some re-planning.

Planning the custom code conversion

Preliminary S/4HANA Readiness Checks via our central ATC system had given us some indication of what all will need to be fixed in our custom code by our team of ABAP developers. While preparing the "cookbooks" mentioned in part 2, we were contemplating dividing the work by simplification items, so that the assigned developer would fix the same issue in many development objects, hopefully gaining momentum while doing so. After some discussions we shelved this plan and decided to divide the work by package, so that a developer would tackle all objects and across simplification items impacted. The main reason for this decision was, to avoid "stepping on each other's toes" and blocking objects with half-baked changes another developer would need to pick up sometime later. Another reason was that we hoped that the work would not be quite as tedious if different simplification items would be in play for a developer while tackling assigned objects.

Getting a list of priority objects in the mix

In parallel to our preparations for the custom code conversion (CCC), the functional and testing teams were running preliminary tests in a converted sandbox system to get an idea of what all no longer worked as it should. As a result of this activity we received a list with objects (programs, function groups and classes) which they deemed priority objects in need of fixing first once we were able to start with the CCC in a converted development system. The list had about 90 objects and we decided to use it for a proof of concept of how to devide up the work and go through the motions of the CCC.

We also decided to tackle and divide this list by main object instead of package to have a simpler relationship between objects and their transport requests. What we want to avoid are dependencies when the time for releasing transports eventually comes, so we opted for (a lot) more but smaller mostly independent transports. We also defined a guideline of how the transport title should look like with a combination of task-ID, fixed keyword (S4HCCC), main object name and initials of the developer working on it. To make it even easier, this concatenated title can be copied from our worklist and should hopefully ensure consistent naming.

We plan to take what we learn from correcting the objects on the priority list and adapt it for the next stage of the project, tackling all the many other objects in need of corrections. Because we have four separate productive systems in play for this conversion, we will prioritize the conversion of objects in the order they are planned to be converted. This for example means that custom code only used in Brazil or in the US doesn't need to be tackled first as they are only relevant for system number 3 to be converted in a few months.

Hitting some snags

SAP's recommendation is to run the S/4HANA Readiness checks directly in the converted system and we already did that with the S4HANA_READINESS_2023 check variant once the latest simplification database had been imported (until you do that you get an error message / tool failure when trying to execute the check). In addition, and while working on objects, we also wanted to make use of the ATC-checks we have been using in our ECC-systems during task release to avoid having "old issues" creep in while fixing S/4HANA issues. As we had the relevant check variant already defined in a central ATC-system (S4HANA 756) I created a new central check combining our task release checks with all the checks active in S4HANA_READINESS_2023.

When I wanted to make use of this check variant, we hit the first snag: a weird error related to the trusted RFC-connection between the checked and the central system which prevented the definition of a check variant via the reference system:

"00024No authorization to log on as a Trusted System (L-RC=1 T-RC=1)."

Our basis team investigated the issue and finally managed to resolve it somehow and I hoped that I would now be able to properly connect the central ATC-system to the S4HANA-Dev system. But, it shouldn't be as now I got a clear error message that our central ATC-system which is on S4HANA 756 cannot provide ATC checks in a system already on 758:

Does the "fun" never end?

Taking a detour to get around these snags

Not being able to make use of our central ATC-system or wait for the system to be upgraded, it became necessary to re-define our main check variant in the S/4HANA development system. In addition, a check variant to define the baseline in that system was also needed to avoid getting inundated in too many unrelated findings. The most tedious task, however, was to adapt the many message priorities based on what we had done in the central system when we set things up there initially. 

Once these settings were in place, I was able to create a local baseline and a default variant which now is the one combining our selected checks with the ones for S/4HANA Readiness. This should make it easy for our development team to execute them often and easily.

Yet another snag

We were however still not "out of the woods" as I noticed that our enhancements - two of which are also on the priority list - were not getting checked as expected but instead produced a tool failure with an error message indicating an inconsistency in the system's namespace settings. I tried setting up check runs for the enhancements but these finished within a few seconds and without doing anything apart from showing the error:

To find out what was happening there, I started a thread in Q&A about "S/4HANA Readiness Check for an enhancement running into "SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1P/ is in scope..." and we were then able to update the entry in table TRNSPACE as needed. When I now open the enhancement via SE84, go to one of the includes and then trigger the ATC-check manually, I'm at least able to get a result. It is however still not possible to include the enhancements in a check run where they are all but ignored from what I can tell, regardless of which objects I add to the selection list.  I have a question about that issue still open here: How to set up an ATC check run for enhancements in an S/4HANA 758 system? 

Apparently, the fun never ends, but this episode certainly does! 

Image generated with Gemini

entry.title: IBM Cloud® Security and Compliance Center Workload Protection for IBM Cloud® VPC and IBM® PowerVS
entry.content:
IBM Cloud® Security and Compliance Center Workload Protection can be used to find and prioritize software vulnerabilities, detect and respond to threats, and manage configurations, permissions, and compliance from source to run. When deploying your architecture in the Cloud, effective protection is crucial for ensuring the health, compliance and early threat detection of your system. 

This feature can be turned on when deploying using the Catalog tiles: IBM® Power Virtual Server with IBM Cloud® VPC landing zone and IBM® Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA®.

Key Features

  •        Real-time visibility: IBM® Power Virtual Server Cloud Security Posture Management. Workload Protection now supports IBM® Power Virtual Server resources as part of its Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) feature. Supported resources includes IBM® Power Virtual Server Workspaces, Instances, Instance Volumes, Networks, Network Security Groups and Public Networks.
  •        Reports: Resource Report generates a CSV that includes Compliance results with the resource detail.

Who is it of interest to?               

IBM Cloud® VPC, IBM® Power Virtual Server and SAP® on IBM® Power Virtual Server Customers 

Benefits

  • Fully automated installation and configuration, add on feature for IBM® Power Virtual Server with IBM Cloud® VPC landing zone and IBM® Power Virtual Server for SAP HANA®,
  • Continuous monitoring of workloads for threats, vulnerabilities, and suspicious behavior.
  • Uses behavioral analysis and threat intelligence to detect attacks in real-time.
  • Simplifies compliance auditing with continuous posture monitoring.
  • Provides reports and evidence for regulatory and internal audits.
  • Centralized dashboard for visibility across multi-cloud and hybrid workloads.
  • Easy management of security policies and incident responses. 

Links

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKB

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKx

 https://ibm.biz/BdnZKR

Featured Image Source: IBM

entry.title: The Final Countdown to SAP S/4HANA: Simplify Your Journey with SDT
entry.content:
With the deadline for SAP ECC extended maintenance approaching, customers are planning their transition to SAP S/4HANA to lower the overall maintenance costs of their business solutions. To secure business support, they must build a compelling business case for a transition strategy that is cost-efficient, minimally disruptive, and tailored to their requirements. 

There are three main paths to transition to SAP S/4HANA: System Conversion, Selective Data Transition (SDT), and New Implementation. Some SAP customers rule out System Conversion due to unacceptable downtime requirements caused by the size of their systems, while others rule out New Implementation because it does not support the migration of historical data—only open items and master data. In these cases, Selective Data Transition offers a practical, tailored and effective approach, enabling customers to move to SAP S/4HANA in a way that meets both technical and business requirements.

Using the SAP Readiness Check, customers can assess whether Selective Data Transition (SDT) is the most suitable approach for their SAP S/4HANA journey, based on several key factors:

  1. Pre-Projects and Prerequisites
    Many SAP S/4HANA transitions require prerequisite projects such as New GL Activation or CVI Implementation. With SDT, these can be integrated into the main project. SDT supports "on-the-fly" solutions such as for CVI conversion, Account-to-Ledger mapping (including document splitting), Chart of Accounts conversion, company code merges or splits, system consolidations, and cost center/profit center restructuring—all delivered within the scope of the project.
  2. Downtime Constraints
    For businesses with strict downtime limitations, SDT offers a shell conversion approach. This enables system upgrades—including simplification item handling and custom code remediation—early in the project. As a result, final cutover is limited to data migration and conversion only, significantly reducing the overall downtime window for the business. Performance optimization opportunities during the project can further shorten the downtime window.
  3. System Size and Data Volume
    Large system sizes—often caused by lack of archiving, redundant data from inactive company codes, or uncontrolled growth—can drive up infrastructure and licensing costs in both on-premise and SAP RISE environments. SDT allows customers to selectively reduce data volume (e.g., by time slicing historical data) based on legal and business retention requirements, helping control future system size and cost.
  4. Business Transformation Requirements
    SDT supports broader business transformation needs, such as financial process changes or structural data harmonization. These changes can be embedded directly into the migration, ensuring the new SAP S/4HANA platform reflects current and future business requirements. Using the Landscape Transformation Validation Framework, customers can decide on the number of test cases that they need to validate data between the ECC and their future SAP S/4HANA system. 

The reasons outlined above are key drivers for why SAP customers choose to transition to SAP S/4HANA using the Selective Data Transition (SDT) approach. Many customers also need to phase their global business transitions—due to specific operational requirements—rather than pursue a full 'Big Bang' system conversion. SDT enables this phased approach by allowing transitions by organizational unit (e.g., company code). Selecting this option requires a detailed rollout plan and dedicated resources from both the customer and their partners to ensure the successful and feasible execution of each rollout go-live. 

How does it work? 

The Mix and Match approach is another SDT option that allows SAP customers to begin with a new SAP S/4HANA system, selectively migrating customizations, developments, and only clean data—historical data included if required.”

How does it work? 

The transition to SAP S/4HANA should not be perceived as a technical deadline, but as a strategic opportunity to provide SAP customers with a stronger, future-ready platform that drives end-to-end business transformation and excellence. 

Delivering a Selective Data Transition (SDT) project gives SAP customers the opportunity to implement key business changes as part of the required data migration/conversion, ensuring their future platform is aligned with their strategic business goals.

For support or to discuss your specific business requirements, please don’t hesitate to reach out or contact DMLT directly via their global inbox sap_dmlt_gce@sap.com .

entry.title: Re: Focused Build Customer Extension Field Changes to Metadata Configuration and Process Type View,
entry.content:
Hi @cgovind can you please provide the mentioned BADI name:

"We can also control which extension fields should be editable/ read-only at process type / field level using both the customization and BAdI which will be explained later in a new blog-post. I will see you there again!!"

 

Ok, i found it:

•In transaction SE18, you have maintained the enhancement spot /SALM/CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD_ENH.
•Choose Display to see several methods that allow you to adjust the custom fields. To see the methods, in the Implementation Example Classes screen, click the entry /SALM/CUST_EXT_FLD_SAMPLE_IMPL.

As a result, the following methods are displayed:◦Method /SALM/IF_CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD~SET_EXT_FLD_EDITABLE_BY_STATUS enables the extension field editability by checking the status.
◦Method /SALM/IF_CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD~SET_CUST_EXT_FC_PROPERTY_VALUE is used to set the field control property based on the status value.
◦Method /SALM/IF_CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD~SET_CUST_EXT_FLD_CHANGEABLE makes the field editable/non-editable in the WebClient UI and in the SAPUI5 application.
◦Method /SALM/IF_CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD~PREVALIDATE_CUSTOMER_FIELD is mainly used to validate the customer fields while importing the requirements using the report /SALM/REQUIREMENT_IMPORT.
◦Method /SALM/IF_CUSTOMER_EXT_FLD~CUSTOMER_FIELD_CONVERSION_EXIT is used to perform conversion exits (external format to internal format) before the record is processed by the report /SALM/REQUIREMENT_IMPORT, for example, adjustment of customer-specific numbers to number range format used in Focused Build.

entry.title: About Camel framework
entry.content:
I am excited to share my insights on Apache Camel Framework that, I hope, will be of use to those who are new to this. It enables integration of various system, applications and data sources to enable smooth flow of information by wrapping the message into a container called Exchange. Below I will be giving description of each component used in the Apache camel framework diagram followed by step-by-step explanation of how the message transfer takes place along with a diagram:

 Components:

  1. Message: It's used to incorporate the data to be transferred. Its has 3 parts:
    1. Header: It's used to send metadata like routing information, API Keys, etc.
    2. Body: Contains the data to be transferred.
    3. Attachment:  Used to attach optional files or binary data to be transferred.
  2. Adapters: It's used to handle the entry and exit points of an integration flow. You can say it acts as a bridge between message and integration flow. They are of 2 types:
    1. Inbound Adapter: It acts as an entry point for message. It incorporates the message into a container called Exchange.
    2. Outbound Adapter: It acts as an exit point for message. It extracts message from Exchange.
  3. Exchange: It acts as a container for the message during the entire routing to the Consumer/Receiver. Structure of the Exchange is as follows:
    1. Exchange ID: It's the Identifier to uniquely identify each Exchange.
    2. MEP (Message Exchange Pattern): It's used to specify whether the message transfer is synchronous or asynchronous. Below is the detailed explanation for each:
      • Synchronous: In this case sender will be expecting will be expecting for a response and there will be 2 message bodies: In-message (for request) and Out-message (for response).
      • Asynchronous: In this sender won't be expecting any response and there will be only one message body: In-Message.
    3. Exception: Used to log any error that occurs during processing.
    4. Property: used to temporarily the values that were fetched, hardcoded or parameterized. The values stored in Properties are accessible within the integration flow.
    5. In-message: It's the original message.
    6. Out-message: It's the response we get in synchronous message transfer.

Message flow step wise explanation:

Step 1: Message from sender is incorporated into Exchange by Inbound Adapter and transferred into the Integration flow.

Step 2: Necessary processing is done to the Message embedded in Exchange in the integration flow.

Step 3: The processed message is extracted by Outbound Adapter and sent to the receiver.

As for the examples, you can follow this links where I have mentioned about the use cases:

  • Currency Conversion using Integration Suite: Currency Conversion using Integration Suite - SAP Community
  • Sending a processed to Receiver: Sending a processed payload to receivers - SAP Community

Hope you like it.

#btp cpi #camel-framework

@Bibekananda123 

@Ankit_Kanungo 

@Mukul_Varshney

entry.title: Merge multiple PDF forms into a single document for download or printing.
entry.content:
Our report required the ability to save or print a bundle of PDF forms, each provided as an entry in an internal table. Initially, we attempted to use the standard approach with the function module FP_GET_PDF_TABLE. However, we encountered limitations specifically, FP_JOB_OPEN was unable to handle the necessary parameters for bundled output in our scenario.

As we did not have the option to modify the Adobe forms themselves or apply relevant SAP Notes, we explored an alternative solution. By leveraging the CL_RSPO_PDF_MERGE class, we successfully implemented PDF merging at runtime. This approach provided a stable and effective way to combine individual PDFs into a single document, ready for download or direct printing.

This is a sample code that provide this functionality:

TYPES: ty_documents_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_document WITH DEFAULT KEY.

FORM generate_merged_pdf USING pt_documents TYPE ty_documents_tab.

  DATA: lt_binary_pdf     TYPE TABLE OF x255,
        lt_merged_pdf     TYPE TABLE OF x255,
        lv_xstring        TYPE xstring,
        lv_merged_xstring TYPE xstring,
        lv_filename       TYPE string,
        lv_path           TYPE string,
        lv_fullpath       TYPE string,
        ls_formoutput     TYPE fpformoutput,
        ls_joboutput      TYPE sfpjoboutput.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Field symbols
*------------------------------------------------------------
  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_document> TYPE ty_document.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* form and doc parametars
*------------------------------------------------------------
  DATA: l_fm_name    TYPE rs38l_fnam,
        fp_docparams TYPE sfpdocparams,
        lv_formname  TYPE fpname.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Set Adobe Form output parameters
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Initialize PDF merger class
*------------------------------------------------------------
  TRY.
      DATA(lo_merger) = NEW cl_rspo_pdf_merge( ).
    CATCH cx_rspo_pdf_merge INTO DATA(lo_error).
      MESSAGE 'PDF merger initialization failed.' TYPE 'E'.
      RETURN.
  ENDTRY.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Open Adobe Forms spool job
*------------------------------------------------------------
  CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_OPEN'
    CHANGING
      ie_outputparams = fp_outputparams
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS          = 1.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Loop through entries and generate individual PDFs
*------------------------------------------------------------
  LOOP AT lt_documents ASSIGNING <fs_document>. 
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Set form name 
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_formname = 'Z_YOUR_PDF_FORM'.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Get generated function module name
*------------------------------------------------------------
    CALL FUNCTION 'FP_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
      EXPORTING
        i_name     = lv_formname
      IMPORTING
        e_funcname = l_fm_name.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Set form parameters
*------------------------------------------------------------
    CLEAR fp_docparams.
    fp_docparams-langu   = sy-langu.
    fp_docparams-country = 'XX'.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Call form generation
*------------------------------------------------------------
    CALL FUNCTION l_fm_name
      EXPORTING
        /1bcdwb/docparams  = fp_docparams
        i_data             = <fs_document>
      IMPORTING
        /1bcdwb/formoutput = ls_formoutput
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS             = 1.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Add document to merger
*------------------------------------------------------------
    lv_xstring = ls_formoutput-pdf.
    lo_merger->add_document( lv_xstring ).
  ENDLOOP.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Close Adobe Forms spool job
*------------------------------------------------------------
  CALL FUNCTION 'FP_JOB_CLOSE'
    IMPORTING
      e_result = ls_joboutput
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 1.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Merge all added PDF documents
*------------------------------------------------------------
  DATA lv_rc TYPE i.
  lo_merger->merge_documents(
    IMPORTING
      merged_document = lv_merged_xstring
      rc              = lv_rc ).

  CHECK lv_rc = 0.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Convert final merged XSTRING to binary
*------------------------------------------------------------
  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer     = lv_merged_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab = lt_merged_pdf.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Prompt user for file location
*------------------------------------------------------------
  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_save_dialog
    CHANGING
      filename = lv_filename
      path     = lv_path
      fullpath = lv_fullpath
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 1.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'File path selection cancelled.' TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.
*------------------------------------------------------------
* Download the merged PDF to selected path
*------------------------------------------------------------
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename = lv_fullpath
      filetype = 'BIN'
    TABLES
      data_tab = lt_merged_pdf.

ENDFORM.

In addition, parameters can be assigned to fp_outputparams to suppress the print dialog or to prefill predefined values, enabling a more streamlined and user-friendly output process. If no parameters are specified, the system will apply the default print settings.

entry.title: SAP CAL systems - Getting started with CAL library Fully Activated Ready to use SAP system
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Appliance Library offers a quick and easy way to create SAP appliances in your cloud infrastructure. With a few clicks you can set up a fully configured demo environment or deploy a standardized system layout for an SAP product based on default or custom SAP software installation stacks.

As we work with SAP partners and customers it is often a comparatively handy tool which can help to be useful in setting something up quickly Adding a few steps on how we got started using it

Start installation

Appliances - SAP Cloud Appliance Library 

  1.  Click on Appliance templates
  2. Search for SAP S/4 HANA Fully Activated option 
  3. Select the appliance  
  4. Choose your cloud provider Azure, AWS or Google  
  5. Click on Create appliance.  

  1. Select create a new account 
  2. Add Name 
  3. Add Cloud Provider 
  4. Add Subscription ID 
  5. Authorization as Standard.  

 

Cost is approx 210 USD per month even when this system is closed. 

It takes approx. 166 mins first time you start using it

In summary, SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL) simplifies the deployment of SAP solutions in the cloud, enabling businesses to quickly set up fully configured demo environments or standardized system landscapes with minimal effort. Whether for testing, training, or development, CAL provides a flexible and efficient way to leverage SAP solutions without the complexities of manual installation. By streamlining access to pre-configured SAP environments, it accelerates innovation and reduces time-to-value, empowering organizations to focus on strategic goals rather than infrastructure setup.

entry.title: Transition to Cloud ALM from SAP Solution Manager 7.2: Approach and Adaption
entry.content:
Transition to Cloud ALM from SAP Solution Manager 7.2: Approach and Adaption

Structure of this blog

This blog is structured to understand the following:

  • Transition to SAP Cloud ALM from Solution Manager
  • Approach for Transition
  • Transition Overview: Cloud ALM vs Solution Manager
  • Approach for Transition using 3 Available Transition options

                             1.Cloud ALM for Operations

                             2.Cloud ALM for Implementation

                             3.Selective Data Transfer Capabilities in Cloud ALM

-Limitations

-Best Practice for Selective Data Transfer

-Preparing Solution Manager for Data Transfer

-Preparing Data Transfer with SAP Readiness Check

-Where goes from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM in SDT

-Library Overview in Cloud ALM      

  • Transition Change Management to Cloud ALM

Transition to SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Solution Manager is in mainstream maintenance until the end of 2027. SAP Cloud ALM will be the ALM platform for all SAP customers. SAP recommends completing the transition to SAP Cloud ALM before this date.

Plan the transition as per your needs, there are 3 possibilities for the transition to SAP Cloud ALM:

 3 options for the transition

Approach for Transition

Transition to Cloud ALM doesn’t happen in a day, it requires planning and execution to reap the benefits of Cloud ALM. Based on the customer requirement we can plan the transition process.

SAP recommends adopting SAP Cloud ALM for service, operations and cloud projects now as it’s ready to use.

For SAP Cloud ALM for implementation adopt in phase-based approach to leverage functionality for Testing, Deployment Management and Solution Documentation which is planned for 2025.

Eventually all the customers will have to make a transition to Cloud ALM as there are no plans for further releases after SAP Solution Manager 7.2.

Transition Planning

Transition Overview - Cloud ALM vs Solution Manager

All the functionalities available in Solution Manager 7.2 might not be available to consume As-Is in Cloud ALM, below is a representation of the Solution Manager 7.2 vs Cloud ALM functionality adoption as per the business use case:

Solution Manager to Cloud ALM mapping

Approach for Transition using 3 Available Transition options

Cloud ALM for Operations:

For leveraging Cloud ALM for operations (Various Monitoring Scenarios) follow the below process:

We can configure below available monitoring scenarios in Cloud ALM for Operations:

Cloud ALM for Implementation:

For leveraging Cloud ALM for implementation follow the below process:

 

We can select the SAP Activate roadmap as per the requirement and start utilizing the roadmap tasks, Q-gate with assigned Scope and Timeboxes.

Using capabilities of Selective Data Transfer in Cloud ALM:

The Selective Data Transfer app provided in SAP Solution Manager offers reports and functionalities for transfer of data to SAP Cloud ALM.

SDT steps from Solution Manager to Cloud ALM

The selective data transfer only allows for initial uploads of data from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM. Modifying previously uploaded data by another upload is not possible. However, it enables us to modify the data during the transfer process.

The selective data transfer follows a spreadsheet-based approach (XLSX). As the upload to Cloud ALM is only supported by XLSX format.

  • Limitations

The selective data transfer process focuses on specific applications in SAP Solution Manager 7.2:

In Scope:

  •  SAP Solution Manager 7.2: Process Management
  •  Focused Build 2.0: Test Step

Out of Scope

  • Requirements Management
  • Change Control Management
  •  IT Service Management
  • Test Management

Reporting Additionally, certain types of data are excluded from the transfer:

  • Alerting and Analytics Library
  • Master data such as landscape and business partners
  • Transactional and project-related data such as test plans, change documents.
  • Analytics e.g. from the Solution Readiness Dashboard.

 

  • SAP Cloud ALM Best Practice for Selective Data Transfer

The Selective Data Transfer (SDT) process consists of two main phases:

Phases in SDT

 

  • Preparing the Solution Manager for Data Transfer

Transaction SM_CALM_DT in SAP Solution Manager (Only applicable for SPS15 and above/ Focused Build 2.0 SP10).

SPS19 and above:

Transaction SM_CALM_DT

SPS 19 and below:

  • Implement SAP Note: 3358439, if the note is not applicable to the Solution Manager system, please upgrade the ST Component to at least SP15.
  • Run the report NOTE_3358439 with option "Step 2: Update & Activate".
  • If ST is SP15 / ST SP16, create the WD application in transaction SE80

  • Activate ICF service of the WD application to access the application. 

Go to transaction SICF and filter by service path "/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/calm_data_transfer". Activate service CALM_DATA_TRANSFER.

  • Selective Data Transfer: Opens the app.

The application contains area for input selection.

  1. 1. From the Selection area, choose the data to be transferred based on selection criteria such as Solution, Branch, Scope, Site, System Role, and Content Language.
  2. Select the type of data be transferred by using the Object Type field.
  3. 3. Choose Ok to generate a table of the selected data in the result area. .(The selected data is automatically grouped into mandatory columns and optional columns).

Once the data quality is verified and finalized, download the selected data to a local storage.

The data is downloaded as a spreadsheet in format (XLSX). If the downloaded data exceeds certain limits, the process for collecting it might run into a timeout error (500 Connection timed out).

Limitations: Value chain diagrams, universal diagrams, and interface diagrams are not included in the download.

  • Preparing the Data Transfer with SAP Readiness Check

SAP Readiness Check analyzes the current SAP Solution Manger 7.2 system and provides a readiness report. It helps to understand which parts of data are ready for the transfer to SAP Cloud ALM. It also highlights any gaps or prerequisites that need to be addressed before initiating the transfer.

In Solution Manager:

  • Execute the program /SDF/RC_ALM_COLLECT_DATA via transaction SA38 in the productive client of the production system of Solution Manager to schedule and download the data archive by performing the following three steps:
    1. Choose Schedule Analysis to schedule the master job to collect data.
    2. Once the data collection jobs finish, choose the Download Analysis Data option from within the program /SDF/RC_ALM_COLLECT_DATA.

In Cloud ALM

Create a “New Analysis” under SAP Readiness Check.

Use the SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM to analyze your current usage of SAP Solution Manager. We can now analyze the usage of Solution Manager data in Cloud ALM and decide for the transition journey.

Refer SAP Note: 3236443 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Readiness Check analyzes the following technical prerequisites:

  • What goes where from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM in SDT

The following table shows which data from which areas in SAP Solution Manager can be transferred to the respective areas in SAP Cloud ALM.

 

 

  • Library Overview in Cloud ALM

The following library options are available on Cloud ALM as on today.

  • Application Library

Under the Application Library, all executable elements from the executable library (solution manager) folder are collected.

  • Configuration Library

Under Configuration Library, all configuration unit originals and their elements from the configuration library folders are collected and displayed in a hierarchical structure.

  • Interface Library

Under Interface Library, all interfaces and composite interfaces, including interface steps from the interface library, are collected and displayed in a hierarchical order.

Transition Change Management to Cloud ALM

  • SAP Cloud ALM offers less customization than SAP Solution Manager, regarding the Post Processing Framework (PPF).
  • We can leverage available APIs to adapt workflows. Workflows can be extended through utilizing external workflow engines like SAP Build Process Automation – Workflow Management.
  • SAP Cloud ALM's transaction type concept is less extensive than Charm’s.
  • We can analyze the current usage and assess whether the current Feature workflow in SAP Cloud ALM suffice the customer needs, if not we can extend the workflow through API’s.

Transition Approaches based on Requirement and Profile

 Conclusion:

  • Use the two solutions side-by-side before moving to Cloud ALM
  •  Let Solution Manager stay in the lead for existing change documents
  •  Use the SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: SearchHelp - Public Cloud Generate UI Service Table
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a custom table in a Public Cloud system.

I have some fields defined with custom domains and fixed values ​​and others defined with standard data elements (such as BUKRS or FAKT).

I published the view with Eclipse using the automatic procedure "Generate Abap Repository object". Tutorial here: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-rap100-generate-ui-service..html

When I launch the published service the fields linked to custom domains with fixed values ​​do not show an automatic searchhelp, the field is a normal text field without the possibility of seeing the list of declared fixed values.

Example fields with Fix Doman Value:

 

The same problems with BUKRS or FAKT data elements. In Bukrs case; I modified the Metadata Extension generated by the tool to insert the SearchHelp:

BUKRS I added this annotation:

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ { entity: { name: 'I_CompanyCodeStdVH', element: 'CompanyCode' } }

But to find "I_CompanyCodeStdVH" isn't easy!

What is the correct or fastest way to:

a) determine the search-help values ​​linked to the standard data elements (usable and released for client use)? Is there a library with all the values ​​cataloged?

b) Is it not possible to use the automatic searchhelp of the fixed domain values? Should a particular annotation be added in the metadata Extension?

Thanks you

entry.title: Reimagining SAP MDG Field Validations Low-Code Approach | UI Field Control Made Dynamic Yet Simple !
entry.content:
Overview

Working with SAP MDG (Master Data Governance) often means balancing two realities: robust governance and agile adaptability. One of the common we face during MDG implementation is managing field validations with user-friendly alerts.

Whether a field should be hidden, mandatory or optional often depends on multiple factors like CR type, entity, company code or user role etc. Traditionally, these validations are implemented via BRF+ expressions, Enhancements in feeder classes, Hard-coded logic in the UI layer/BAdIs.

The Problem: Code Dependency in Field Validation

MDG validations are crucial for maintaining clean and consistent master data. However, the standard methods come with limitations & most teams rely on:

  • Feeder class logic: Feeder class enhancements require ABAP expertise and testing cycles.
  • UI rule maintenance is scattered and often difficult to track.
  • BRF+ rules or hardcoded validations code: Every new business scenario (e.g. different behaviour based on company code or region) means new logic and testing.
  • No centralized view of how validations are controlled across the application.

- While these are functional, they’re rigid, developer-dependent, and non-scalable. This complexity makes it difficult for functional/ business teams to adapt validations quickly and every change creates a dependency on development teams reducing agility.

Challenges: Too Much Code, Too Little Agility
  • Hide/Show fields based on Role UI logic  is non-transparent.
  • Maintain field rules over time - Static logic -No visibility for business teams.
  • The key pain point: Every small validation change turns into a development cycle.

- But what if we could avoid coding and development cycle every time specially for simple level properties or validations, yet still provide fully dynamic, context-sensitive field behaviour.

Solution: Rule the Config-Driven Field Validation Framework [ All In One Place]

To address these challenges, a custom configuration table was designed to serve as a central validation engine covers easy level validations. Field behaviour across various MDG entities and scenarios was defined through this table eliminating the need for code changes every time.

- These rules are consumed at runtime ensuring the UI responds dynamically based on master data context.

- Validation Types : M = Mandatory, O = Optional, H = Hidden, R = Read Only

- This table is maintained via a simple maintenance view or SM30 transaction/T-Code assigned to TMG accessible to key users or functional consultants.

All this happens without modifying feeder classes or enhancing the UI every time.

Benefits of This Approach
  • No More Coding: New validation rules don’t require any ABAP changes at-least for simple validations.
  • Business Empowerment: Functional/Business teams can maintain rules via a user-friendly view.
  • Central Governance: One place to view and manage all field control rules along with by whom & when it’s added or changed.
  • Audit-friendly: clearly see why and when a rule applies.
  • No BRF+ overload: clean separation of decision logic.
  • Faster Change Cycles: No transports or regression testing required for every new validation rule.
  • Scalable to all MDG domains MDG – S,M,C (Supplier, Material, Customer)
Real Impact
  • After rollout: Reduced ~70% of validation-related change requests.
  • Built an enterprise-grade, reusable framework used across modules.
  • Delivered faster time-to-compliance for changing regulatory/market needs.
  • It's a shift from “write code to validate” ➝ to “configure to adapt”
How It Works: Plug-In Logic, Zero Enhancement for later changes
  1. Custom Table is maintained in SM30 or Z/Y T-code (or via custom UI).
  2. Lightweight enhancement logic is added once to retrieve rules based on runtime values.
  3. The UI behaviour adjusts: field becomes hidden, read-only mandatory or optional.
  4. No need to touch feeder classes again & ever.
You can even combine this with:
  • Workflow context (field is mandatory only in approval steps)
  • User or Role-level personalization (validation based on user group)
  • For multiple conditions can add different Sr. No conditions. E.g. Department is mandatory for Company Code =  ‘0001’  & '0005', So Can add two records with different Sr. No.
Requirement - For CR Type: ZCCT1P2: Create Cost Center with Hry. Assignments:
  1.    Business Area to be hidden.
  2.    Profit Center is mandatory.
  3.    Department is mandatory for Company Code =  ‘0001’
  4.    User Responsible is mandatory for cost center category = ‘%’.
Implementation

[ Fig. 1.0. Table Contents ]

  • ZCCT1P2: Create Cost Center with Hry. Assignments.
  • Step ‘0’ defines Requestor Level.
  • Tech Property  & Conditional Tech-Property stores technical attributes.
  • Table also contains audit columns to track changes.
  1. BAdI: USMD_ACC_FLD_PROP_CUST_DEP_SET
  • EI: ZFI_ES_MDG_FLD_PROP
  • BAdI Implementation: ZFI_MDG_FLD_PROP
  • Class: ZCL_FI_MDG_FLD_PROP
  • Method: IF_EX_USMD_ACC_FLD_PROP_CDS~MODIFY_FLD_PROP_ATTR
  METHOD if_ex_usmd_acc_fld_prop_cds~modify_fld_prop_attr.

    DATA:lr_model TYPE REF TO if_usmd_app_context.
    CALL METHOD cl_usmd_app_context=>get_context
      RECEIVING
        eo_context = lr_model.
    IF lr_model IS BOUND.
      CALL METHOD lr_model->get_attributes
        IMPORTING
          ev_crequest_type = DATA(lv_cr_type)
          ev_crequest_step = DATA(lv_cr_step).
    ENDIF.

    DATA: go_tab_descr   TYPE REF TO cl_abap_tabledescr,
          go_struc_descr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
          lt_components  TYPE abap_component_tab.
    DATA: name_range TYPE RANGE OF zfi_mdg_valt-condition_tech.

    "Pre-Exception Handling To Get Valid Fields For it_data As not-valid entry entered in Condition Property
    go_tab_descr ?= cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_data ).
    CHECK sy-subrc = 0.
    go_struc_descr ?= go_tab_descr->get_table_line_type( ). "Get the structure of your internal table
    lt_components  = go_struc_descr->get_components( ).
    REFRESH name_range.
    name_range = VALUE #( FOR wa IN lt_components ( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = wa-name ) ).

    "Check Mandatory(*)/Hide/Read Only/Optional Attribute Property
    SELECT * FROM zfi_mdg_valt INTO TABLE (it_mdg_val)
          WHERE cr_type = _cr_type AND cr_step = _cr_step AND entity_type = _entity
          AND zmodule = '0G' AND active = 'X'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.

      LOOP AT ct_fld_prop ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data_2>).
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'USMD_FP' OF STRUCTURE <fs_data_2> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_fld_prop_2>).

        LOOP AT it_mdg_val INTO DATA(wa_val) WHERE entity_type = iv_entity. "Traverse Custom Defined Properties

          IF wa_val-condition_tech IS NOT INITIAL. "Check If Condition maintained or free of any condition
            IF  wa_val-condition_tech IN name_range.    "Preventive Action
              READ TABLE it_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data_con>)
              WITH KEY (wa_val-condition_tech) = wa_val-condition_val.
              "Condition is not matching, continue with next iteration of properties of it_mdg_val
              IF sy-subrc NE 0.
                CONTINUE.
              ENDIF.
            ELSE.
              CONTINUE.   "Not Valid Condition Attribute/Property
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.

          "Check for further tech property
          ASSIGN COMPONENT wa_val-tech_name OF STRUCTURE <fs_fld_prop_2> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_field_2>).
          IF <ls_field_2> IS ASSIGNED.
            <ls_field_2> = wa_val-property.
          ENDIF.
          UNASSIGN  <ls_field_2>.
        ENDLOOP.

        UNASSIGN <fs_fld_prop_2>.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.
       2.  BAdI: USMD_RULE_SERVICE
  • EI: ZFI_ES_MDG_VAL
  • BAdI Implementation: ZFI_MDG_VAL
  • Class: ZCL_FI_MDG_VAL
  • Method: IF_EX_USMD_RULE_SERVICE~CHECK_ENTITY
  METHOD if_ex_usmd_rule_service~check_entity.

    DATA: lr_model   TYPE REF TO if_usmd_app_context.
    DATA: go_tab_descr   TYPE REF TO cl_abap_tabledescr,
          go_struc_descr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
          lt_components  TYPE abap_component_tab.
    DATA: name_range TYPE RANGE OF zfi_mdg_valt-condition_tech.

    CALL METHOD cl_usmd_app_context=>get_context
      RECEIVING
        eo_context = lr_model.
    IF lr_model IS BOUND.
      CALL METHOD lr_model->get_attributes
        IMPORTING
          ev_crequest_type = DATA(lv_cr_type)
          ev_crequest_step = DATA(lv_cr_step).
    ENDIF.

    "Pre-Exception Handling To Get Valid Fields For it_data As not-valid entry entered in Condition Property
    go_tab_descr ?= cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_data ).
    CHECK sy-subrc = 0.
    go_struc_descr ?= go_tab_descr->get_table_line_type( ). "Get the structure of your internal table
    lt_components  = go_struc_descr->get_components( ).
    REFRESH name_range.
    name_range = VALUE #( FOR wa IN lt_components ( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = wa-name ) ).

    "Mandatory Check Working for FI : GL,CC,PC
    SELECT * FROM zfi_mdg_valt INTO TABLE (it_zfi_mdg_valderv)
      WHERE cr_type = _cr_type AND cr_step = _cr_step
      AND zmodule = '0G' AND property = 'M' AND active = 'X'.

    LOOP AT it_zfi_mdg_valderv INTO DATA(wa_val).
      LOOP AT it_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_data>).
        IF <ls_data> IS ASSIGNED.

          IF wa_val-condition_tech IS NOT INITIAL. "Check If Condition maintained or free of any condition
            IF  wa_val-condition_tech IN name_range.    "Preventive Action

              ASSIGN COMPONENT wa_val-condition_tech OF STRUCTURE <ls_data> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_con>).
              IF <ls_con> IS ASSIGNED AND <ls_con> IS NOT INITIAL.
                IF <ls_con> NE wa_val-condition_val.
                  CONTINUE.
                ENDIF.
                UNASSIGN <ls_con>.
              ENDIF.

            ELSE.
              APPEND VALUE #( msgty = 'E' msgv1 = 'Please check entity name' msgv2 = wa_val-condition_tech   ) TO et_message.
              CONTINUE.
              "msgid = 'zfi_mdg_message'  msgno = 000
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.

          ASSIGN COMPONENT wa_val-tech_name OF STRUCTURE <ls_data> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_value>).
          IF <ls_value> IS ASSIGNED AND <ls_value> IS INITIAL.
            APPEND VALUE #( msgid = wa_val-msgid msgty = wa_val-msgty
            msgno = wa_val-msgno msgv1 = wa_val-msgv1 msgv2 = wa_val-msgv2 ) TO et_message.
            UNASSIGN <ls_value>.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.


  ENDMETHOD.
Output
  1. Scenario I

       1.1. With Loading, Business area is hidden, Profit Center is mandatory, Department & User Responsible are not mandatory.

[ Fig 1.1 : Initial Screen ]

1.2.  With entering Company code = ‘0001’, Department becomes mandatory (*).

[ Fig 1.2 : Department is mandatory ]

2. Scenario II

2.1. For Company code = ‘0003’, Department is not mandatory

[ Fig 2.1 : Department not mandatory ]

3. Scenario III

3.1. With loading, User Responsible is not mandatory by default.

[ Fig 3.1 : User Responsible not mandatory ]

3.2. With Cost center category = ‘%’, User Responsible becomes mandatory.

[ Fig 3.2 : User Responsible mandatory ]

3.3. Click on Check: As Profit Center & User Responsible are blank, we got error messages defined in our Z table.

[ Fig 3.3 : Messages ]

3.4.Errors on Check

[ Fig 3.4 : Messages ]

4. Additional Sanity Check:

To troubleshoot scenario where user has maintained conditional-tech-property/attribute as not-valid property value ( By mistake ; ) So below error will appear on hitting Check.

As we are using this conditional-tech-property to read IT_DATA, it’s safe idea to check pre-hand whether the property is valid and actually present in IT_DATA to refrain from last moment surprises. (or technically a dump ; )

[ Fig. 4.1. Preventive Code ]

[ Fig : 4.2. conditional tech attribute = ‘CCODECCTR1’ is not valid attribute of data model ]

5. Message Class: Generic 000, So No transport required every time for new messages.

[ Fig : 5 Message Class ]

Conclusion

- SAP MDG is built to empower governed, agile master data. But flexibility shouldn’t mean fragility. With a configurable validation engine, we’ve transformed how we think about field behavior turning a developer-led task into a business-led control mechanism and maximum governance.

Thanks !

entry.title: Re: Can Tax Code Be Changed Without Reversal in S/4HANA P...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Unfortunately, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, once a supplier invoice is posted, you can't change the tax code even if the tax amount is correct. This is because tax codes affect legal and financial reporting, so SAP locks them in to ensure compliance and a clean audit trail.

So what can you do ?

Option 1: Reverse and repost the invoice (this is the proper way)

This is SAP’s standard process:

  • You reverse the original invoice (using the “Manage Supplier Invoices” app, for example).
  • Then, you create a new invoice with the correct tax code.

Yes, it’s a bit of extra work, but it’s the only way to ensure your tax reporting stays accurate and compliant.

 

Option 2: Post a manual adjustment (only in some cases)

If the tax amount is correct and you’re only concerned about the tax code for internal purposes (like analytics), you could:

  • Leave the original invoice as-is.
  • Post a manual journal entry to reclassify the tax amounts between accounts.

Just note: this won’t change the tax code on the original invoice, and it won’t show up correctly in your VAT reports. So this is more of a workaround for internal corrections—not for anything that affects tax filings.

 

What you can’t do :

  • There’s no way to edit the tax code on a posted invoice.
  • No app, no API, no back-end workaround for that in the public cloud version.
  • Customizing isn’t possible either it’s a locked field after posting.

 

In short:

If the tax code is wrong, even if the amount is right, you’ll need to reverse and repost the invoice to fix it properly.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Automatic PO not created in S/4 HANA Public Cloud aft...
entry.content:
Hello, 

if I were you, I would check the following: 

1. Workflow or Configuration Issues

  • Flexible Workflow Not Configured: Automatic PO creation often depends on the correct setup of flexible workflows for RFQs and supplier quotations in S/4HANA Public Cloud. If the workflow is not configured or not triggered, the PO will not be created automatically.

  • Release Strategy: The RFQ or quotation may require approval before a PO can be generated. Ensure that the workflow allows for automatic release or that all necessary approvals are completed.

2. Integration and Master Data Settings

  • Missing or Incorrect Integration Settings: Ensure that the integration scenario between Ariba Sourcing and S/4HANA Public Cloud is fully configured, including communication arrangements, system connections, and relevant user authorizations.

  • Master Data Prerequisites: The material master and vendor master records must be set to allow automatic PO creation. This includes:

    • "Automatic PO" flag enabled in both material and vendor master records.

    • Source list and purchase info records maintained for the material and vendor combination.

3. Process and Data Consistency

  • Quotation Data Issues: If the awarded quotation contains inconsistencies (e.g., missing or mismatched GL accounts, currencies, or required fields), the PO creation may fail silently or trigger errors.

  • Budget or Commitment Issues: In some cases, budget or commitment management settings may prevent PO creation if commitments are not properly reversed or managed.

Hope it will help you.

Best regards,

Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Re: 批量运行结算WBS
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Yes, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as of now, the replacement app "Run Settlement: Actual" indeed only supports single project execution.

For batch settlement, currently there is no standard Fiori app that supports mass execution of project settlement in the same centralized way as the deprecated app did.

However, you have two possible alternatives:

  1. Use Background Job Scheduling (App: "Schedule Project Settlement - Actuals")
    • You can schedule settlement as a recurring job for multiple projects using selection criteria (e.g. project type, status).
    • This is found under "Schedule Project Settlement - Actuals" or within the "Job Schedule for Projects" app.
    • Go to App "Schedule General Ledger Jobs" > Choose Job Template "Project - Settlement: Actual".
  2. Create a Custom CDS View + RAP App (Advanced)
    • If mass execution is essential in your process, your technical team can build a custom RAP-based app or automation that loops through multiple WBS elements using available APIs.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or P...
entry.content:
Hello @Nithesh_Yadav 

Thanks for the detailed context and screenshots. You're not the first to run into this; it’s a common blocker when setting up SAP Mobile Start via the SAP Start booster under the Foundation Plan.

Solution

1st option,

Upgrade to Standard Plan (Recommended)

  • Gives you access to Content Manager.
  • Let’s you import Fiori content and assign it to users.
  • Apps will then show up correctly in both SAP Start and Mobile Start.

2nd option,

Manual Workaround (Not ideal)

  • You can try manually creating tiles and linking S/4HANA apps via URL.
  • But it’s time-consuming, doesn’t scale well, and Mobile Start may not recognise them

Summary

Issue

Explanation

No apps showing

Foundation Plan doesn’t support content federation or assignments

Booster is successful, but empty UI

Booster sets up trust & connection, not app visibility

SAP Mobile Start empty

Because apps aren’t federated or assigned via Work Zone

Solution

Upgrade to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan

 

Visit these SAP useful sites Below

Learning Journey – SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition

SAP Blog – Real-World Setup

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Technical Error when calling Project Element Badi
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you're getting "Technical Error when Calling Project Element BAdI" when changing the Project Manager, it likely means there's a problem in a custom BAdI implementation triggered during that change.

Here's how to troubleshoot:

1. Go to the “Custom Logic” app and check any BAdIs related to project elements or project header. Look for custom code that might be failing.

2. Use the “Application Log” app to find more details about the error (search by project or time).

3. Check if the new Project Manager is valid (e.g., is set up as a Business Partner with the right roles).

4. If needed, temporarily disable the custom logic and try again—if the error goes away, the issue is in the BAdI.

5. Review the code for missing checks or unhandled exceptions.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Address error Germany: “Max length 6 of target field exceeded. Source field address_information stat
entry.content:
As we all know we have different countries supported by EC and we can select different countries in EC address. Depending on the country selected the address fields are populated.

While doing replication of address for EC to ECP it is necessary to have the length to be defined as per SAP standards in EC also it is necessary to have fields mapped as per standard. This does not mean we cannot add custom fields it can be done but than as always it is better to keep it simple by using standard fields.

We have used all standard fields in EC for country Germany and the same have been mapped to ECP.However while replicating employee from EC to ECP we were receiving the error ““Max length 6 of target field exceeded. Source field address_information state DEURhineland-Palatinate”

First look at this error and we did check in “Manage business Configuration” and there was no field called “state” defined for Germany.

Then we did get puzzled of how we are getting an error with a field being referred which is not enabled.

Next step we did was pull in a report from reporting .We came to know the beauty of the reports when we downloaded the report . The report showed that during imports the value in field state was being updated which resulted in above error. On removing the data from “state” and reimporting in EC and running replication the error was solved

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start via SAP Start Booster – No Apps or P...
entry.content:
Hi @Chuma,

Thank you so much for the detailed and structured response , this really helps clarify things!

We suspected that the Foundation Plan's limitations around content federation and role assignments were likely the root cause, and your confirmation aligns perfectly with our findings.

We had hoped the SAP Start booster would take care of all required configurations, including app visibility, but as you rightly pointed out, it seems the booster handles trust and connectivity, not content provisioning or launchpad content assignments - which require Content Manager, only available in the Standard Plan.

We did consider the manual workaround (creating tiles with target mappings via URLs) but as you mentioned, it’s not sustainable for a scalable setup—especially in a production-grade mobile enablement scenario via SAP Mobile Start.

We will now evaluate the upgrade to SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Plan, as it appears to be the cleanest and most maintainable solution moving forward.

Really appreciate you taking the time to share your insights and confirm the technical boundaries here.

Thanks again!
Nithesh T S

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day Bangalore -- Connection, Gratitude, AI, and Local Engineering Pride
entry.content:
The first-ever SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Bangalore was a tremendous success, bringing together 200+ participants for what became the largest SAP Fiori Innovation Day yet. This event provided local developers an opportunity to connect with customers, showcase our latest AI-powered innovations, and help us understand where to focus our development efforts.

In this post, I'll discuss the key sessions, share insights from our presentations, and highlight the remarkable contributions from our local development community.

Engineering Pride from Our Local Contributions to the UX Foundation Portfolio

We started with Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, providing our UX strategy overview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. His vision about intelligent UX and the humanness aspect that creates customer loyalty resonated strongly with our audience.

Ioannis Grammatikakis, SVP for UX Foundation at SAP, presenting the UX strategy and vision for SAP S/4HANA

We then demonstrated the latest AI functionality embedded across the SAP S/4HANA portfolio:

  • My Home: AI-powered app recommendations, intelligent insights cards, and Joule integration for personalized experiences
  • List Report: Natural language filtering that translates user requests into precise filter criteria
  • Object Page: AI summarization features allowing users to select sections and share results with colleagues

It was gratifying to see our local Bangalore team members demonstrating these features they directly contributed to developing. The audience engagement was particularly strong, with participants asking about integrating S/4HANA systems with Microsoft Teams services and bot platforms.

Hands-on Experience with Hands-on Experience with SAP Fiori Tools

Our hands-on sessions led by Gaurav Rawat showed how SAP Fiori tools in Business Application Studio (BAS) addresses 80% of common use cases. Participants experienced the complete development cycle from template selection through deployment, with tools automatically handling metadata, annotations, and dependencies. The interactive nature of the sessions sparked excellent questions from participants, including how to configure simple filters in SAPUI5 Freestyle applications, setting up SAP Fiori tools extensions in BAS, and whether SAP Fiori apps developed through SAP Fiori tools can be containerized using Docker.

Participants working with SAP Fiori tools and exploring AI-powered development features

Interactive Demo Booths: Hands-on Exploration

Beyond the main stage presentations, participants had the opportunity to explore multiple demo booths throughout the day, each focusing on different aspects of SAP Fiori development and user experience:

Leverage SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Participants explored how to identify apps strategic for their business needs by leveraging the comprehensive library of available SAP Fiori applications and learning about installation options.

Intelligent Entry Experience to SAP S/4HANA: Demonstrations showcased the journey from first steps to daily excellence, featuring the latest My Home page in SAP S/4HANA systems, which serves as the standard entry page providing a personalized and unified user experience.

Seamless and Collaborative Experience with Technology-Agnostic Cards: This session focused on creating seamless user interactions through technology-agnostic cards that enhance collaboration and overall application usability.

AI-Assisted Easy Filter: A deep dive into redefining the filtering experience through innovation, featuring natural language filtering capabilities and AI-powered search functionality.

SAP Fiori Elements Fast-Track App Development: Best practices and strategies for boosting developer efficiency and building applications that can grow with business needs using SAP Fiori elements. Participants were particularly interested in the availability of public documentation for these app generation capabilities.

Interactive Demo Booths

Mindset's AI-Powered Innovation: Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience

Our partner Mindset Consulting presented their session "Reimagining SAP Fiori: AI-Powered User Experience" showcasing their innovative document summary generator using LLM models. Their solution demonstrates how AI can automatically process and summarize documents, with the generated summaries being stored in HANA DB for efficient retrieval and reuse. This demonstration highlighted how partner solutions can extend SAP Fiori applications with advanced AI capabilities, showing practical applications of intelligent document processing in enterprise scenarios.

Developing Applications is a Team Sport, So Connections Matter

Modern enterprise applications require collaboration between business owners, user researchers, designers, enterprise architects, and developers—with AI tools increasingly becoming team members. The diverse skills of our Bangalore participants perfectly matched today's fusion development teams. The energy and engagement throughout the event will directly influence our future innovations.

SAP Fiori Innovation Day team

Gratitude for Making SAP Fiori Innovation Day a Huge Success

This event's success came from the energy and engagement of 200+ participants. Their questions, comments, and feedback provide invaluable guidance for future innovations.

Thanks to all SAP presenters for their valuable contributions. Special thanks to Mindset Consulting for sponsoring and their presentation on Reimagining SAP Fiori User Experience, and the SAP Inside Track team for integrating our UX track into their larger community event.

Watch for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Your Region

  • Dallas, Texas, USA -- October 17, 2025 as part of the ASUG chapter meeting
  • Paris, France -- December 10, 2025

Continue Your Learning

  • Subscribe to the bi-monthly SAP Fiori Development Newsletter
  • Sign up for the SAP S/4HANA UX Roundtable in India (starting in summer 2025)
  • Join the monthly SAP Fiori Development Roundtable for U.S. and Europe if that fits your schedule better

entry.title: Re: Quantity field is not updating in ACDOCA table during...
entry.content:
Hi @msshihab1987 ,

Please refer to KBA note 27922 - Info: Settlement types and quantity settlement - SAP for Me

If account-based profitability analysis is used, the logic for updating consumption quantities is the same as described above...

...the system updates primary cost element quantities and activity quantities if these are settled according to the cost element or if the settlement cost element has a unit of measure. If only the quantities for the revenue elements are required in the profitability analysis, then only the revenue elements should be settled according to the cost element because this generates a large amount of data.

I hope it helps.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: Add header text to uploading budget template PSM
entry.content:
I’m reaching out for assistance with an issue we’re facing in our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment (Public Sector scope).

We’re currently uploading budgets for specific cost centers using the “Public Sector: Run Application Job” functionality. The job runs successfully and the budget documents appear in the “Manage Budget Documents” app. However, when we go to the Manage Budget Documents app, we see that the document description in the header is always set to “Generated Automatically” (see attached screenshot). We would like to add our own description during the upload (For example: Budget round June 2025), so that the documents are easier to recognize afterwards. 

 

So far, I’ve tried the following:

 

  1. Adding a custom column in the upload template → either ignored or causes an error
  1. Renaming the job title → only shows in the job log, not as the document header text
  1. Creating budget documents manually via the app → works, but not feasible for bulk uploads
  1. Looking into APIs or extensibility options → seems overly complex or unavailable in standard public cloud

 

My questions:

 

  1. Is there a standard way to include a document header text when uploading budgets via the application job?
  1. If so, how can this be configured or enabled?
  1. If not, what would be the best alternative to ensure uploaded documents are easily distinguishable?

 

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Solution Quotation Management (7HB)
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, we have delivered an SAP Best Practices scenario for Solution Quotation Management.

7HB - Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ 

The SAP S/4HANA Quotation Management with SAP CPQ offers customers the capability to create quotations for sales and service contract products in SAP CPQ. After the customer accepts the proposal the SAP CPQ Quote creates Sales Order and Service Contract automatically. The linkage document in between is the SAP Solution Quote document, which holds information about SAP CPQ Quote ID and the document ID of Sales Order and Service Contract.

(fig 1. SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA )

Let’s start by looking in some more detail at the SAP Configure, Price, Quote integration with Solution quotation Management in SAP S/4HANA , Private Edition. SAP CPQ enables salespeople to create complex product configurations with tailored pricing and customized attributes with ease. The seamless pre-defined integration of SAP CPQ and solution quotation  in SAP S/4HANA  is implemented via BTP Cloud Integration and encompasses the transfer of pricing and product configuration data via CPS, and master data replication via MDI. The new scope item titled Solution Quotation Management with SAP CPQ (7HB) has been created for which contains the standard mapping logic required for implementing the integration.

(fig 2 SAP CPQ Integration with Solution Order Management process flow diagram)


The integration enables salespeople to leverage the full set of CPQ capabilities to create customized quotes. In the SAP CPQ front end, the order is configured and calculated based on the selection of available attributes. Once the quote is confirmed by the customer and the order is placed, it is handed over to solution order management in SAP S/4HANA  for further processing.  When the Solution Order has been processed, the status in CPQ is also updated to show that the order has been placed for fulfilment. The integration initially supports configurable and non-configurable sales and service contract products, with further product types to be added in future releases. Via the standard OData API, the initial creation, as well as read, update and delete operations are supported.

How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

We would like to provide you with a comprehensive overview of how and where you can raise your voice to influence developments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition going forward.

For more information, see the blog post from Anja Wilde here.

For general roadmap information please check the SAP S/4HANA Roadmap.

 Thanks for reading this blog post.

If you are also interested in other Lines of Business and Industries for this new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 release, I would like to draw your attention to my link collection blog – The Link Collection.

If you want to learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, join our Partner Community. This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates concerning the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

For more information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, check out the following links:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release info: sap.com/s4hana
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community here
  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition here
  • Help Portal Product Page here
  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here

entry.title: Re: MDGF- How to edit a GL account
entry.content:
Hello @former_member836676 , 

Don't go Mass change option to edit a GL account. 
To edit GL in MDG, First search for the existing GL account in NWBC-> Financial Accounting->Search G/L Account Centrally. 
It will display the list of the GL accounts. 
Now, open the GL account you want to edit and you can see edit option beside each category(Account Details, Account Descriptions, etc.,) . 

Click on edit, and it will ask for the CR type and edition


Now select your required CR type, Edition and you can edit the GL account data and submit the CR for approval. 
Thanks, 
Rajeshwar

entry.title: Request for setting default field value in purchase orders
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are working in the S4/HANA Public Cloud environment and would like to configure default values for specific fields when creating a purchase order (PO). We are specifically looking to prefill fields such as:

- Company code 

- Purchasing group 

We have already reviewed the options under Set User Defaults, but these fields are either unavailable there or not taking effect during PO creation. For instance, i filled in "001" as default value for Purchasing group without any result.

Could you please advise us:

1. Whether it's possible to achieve this through configuration or user personalization in Public Cloud?

2. If not, what is the recommended way to set such default fields?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: PL30 entries missing after copying Cons Group in SAP Group Reporting on premise
entry.content:
Hi,

We created a Consolidation Group (A02) as a copy of an existing Consolidation Group (A01), using the SAP Group Reporting Fiori app > "Consolidation Group Create" > "Copy From".

This new Cons Group (A02) has got all entries of the original group (A01) but the PL30 entries are missing. How do I copy the PL30 entries from the original group to the new group ? We do not want to re-execute the Cons Monitor tasks in A02 to generate the PL30 entries, as the original CG (A01) has entries for cons units of prior years and periods which are audited and locked.  

We did a export of all PL30 entries from A01 and when we try to upload the journals using the Import Group Journal entries app into A02, the system is not allowing to post entries with document types 3B, 38, P1, P2 and P3 which are defined as automatic postings.

Please guide, how to do this copy activity, to get the PL30 entries as well, without re-executing the Cons Monitor tasks.  

regards,

Indraneel

I need to design GL account bank as follow:

Sample GL Account code

1000003 Bank outgoing (Use the account code for post outgoing payment)

Dr. 1000002 Bank incoming

Sample Import Bank Statement (Manage Incoming Payment Files):

Dr. 1000001  Bank Balance

Chanaporn S.

entry.title: Re: Taking online exam with seperate device for webcam
entry.content:
Hi @AaronANZ 

It has to be on the same machine as outlined in Getting certified - FAQs "What are the technical requirements for taking an exam?", however it can also be an external webcam that is connected to the laptop via USB.
It cannot be a mobile phone as mobile phones are not allowed to be used during the exam. so the advice is to get an external webcam if the built-in webcam does not work and connect it to the laptop via USB.
Kind regards
Margit
